title: Promoter Motif Pattern Analysis - Gene Type Correlation
description: Analysis of 6 mRNA sequences reveals significant variation in promoter motif counts (TATA, polyA, CCAAT) across different gene types. BRCA2 shows extreme motif counts (8x TATA, 13x polyA, 11x CCAAT), while FAS and BRCA1 show minimal TATA boxes (1-2x). High polyA signal counts (6-13x) appear in multiple genes including BRCA1, BRCA2, and C6. ATP7A shows unusually high CCAAT box count (7x) with low polyA signals (3x).

title: Gene Length Correlation with Promoter Motif Counts
description: Analysis of 7 mRNA sequences reveals significant correlation between gene length and promoter motif abundance. Longer genes show higher counts of TATA boxes, polyA signals, and CCAAT boxes. PTEN (8515 bp) shows highest TATA count (13x), BRCA2 appears to have highest polyA/CCAAT counts despite not analyzed in this session. Shorter genes like C6 (3691 bp) show minimal TATA boxes (1x). This suggests longer genes may require more regulatory elements for proper transcription initiation and processing

Also called Hero11 (heat-resistant obscure protein 11), suggesting stress response function. Recent bioinformatic analysis links Hero proteins to cardiovascular disease pathogenesis (PMID:40459864). Detected in esophageal squamous cell carcinoma progression (PMID:41
evidence: BLAST: 100% human self-match, 77-97% mammalian conservation, 60% zebrafish homolog. PubMed: 3 key papers linking to proliferation, mTORC1, cancer, CVD. Gene aliases: L10K, Hero11, HSPC023, LYDG10.

title: C6orf100 (LINC01556-encoded micropeptide) - Primate-specific regulatory peptide
description: C6orf100 encodes a 62 aa uncharacterized protein from the LINC01556 lncRNA locus. Sequence is Arg-rich (14.5%) with balanced charge/hydropathy, suggesting potential nucleic acid binding or intrinsically disordered function. BLAST shows no homologs beyond human, indicating primate-specific or rapidly evolving peptide. LINC01556 is implicated as biomarker in diabetic retinopathy (PMID:33221518) and ovarian cancer (PMID:37528285), but the micropeptide's molecular function remains completely unknown
evidence: UniProt Q5JQF7: 62 aa, Arg-rich (9/62). BLAST: only human self-match (100% identity, no species homologs). PubMed: LINC01556 linked to disease biomarkers but peptide function uncharacterized.

title: C8orf48 - Mammalian-conserved uncharacterized protein
description: C8orf48 encodes a 319 aa uncharacterized protein on chromosome 8p22. Sequence shows balanced charge/hydropathy (33.9% charged, 31.0% hydrophobic). BLAST reveals mouse homolog with 57.2% identity (E-value 9.26e-94), indicating functional conservation across mammals. Despite this conservation, the protein remains uncharacterized with no known domains or molecular function. Represents a true dark gene - conserved but functionally unexplored.
evidence: NCBI NM_001007090.3: 1420 bp mRNA, 319 aa ORF. UniProt Q96LL4: uncharacterized. BLAST: mouse homolog 57.2% identity. Composition: balanced charged/hydrophobic residues.

title: C8orf48 Hypothesis - Nuclear regulatory protein
description: Based on sequence characteristics (balanced charge/hydropathy, no transmembrane domains) and mammalian conservation (57% identity with mouse), C8orf48 likely functions as a nuclear regulatory protein, possibly involved in chromatin organization or transcriptional regulation. The high conservation despite lacking known domains suggests it may be a scaffold protein or interact with conserved partners. Its chromosome 8p22 location is not in a fragile site region, and the balanced amino acid composi
evidence: 319 aa, balanced composition (33.9% charged), mouse homolog 57.2% identity (E=9.26e-94), no transmembrane prediction, no known Pfam domains, uncharacterized in UniProt.

title: KIAA0051 is IQGAP1
description: D29640/KIAA0051 shows 100% identity to IQGAP1 (Ras GTPase-activating-like protein). This 1657aa protein is highly conserved across mammals (96% mouse identity) and has homologs in IQGAP2/3 paralogs. Remote homologs found in yeast (rng2) and Dictyostelium (rgaA, gapA), indicating ancient evolutionary origin. IQGAP proteins regulate cell signaling, cytoskeleton, and cell adhesion.
evidence: BLAST: 100% identity to IQGAP1 (E=0, Score=3427), 96% to mouse IQGAP1, 59% to human IQGAP3, significant hits to fungal IQGAP homologs

title: C5orf17 - poorly characterized small protein

Protein homologs found in primates (gorilla, chimpanzee, gibbon) but with varying lengths (77-94aa), suggesting poor conservation or annotation issues. PubMed links to cancer biomarkers (oxaliplatin neuropathy, lung squamous cell carcinoma prognosis) and rheumatoid arthritis lncRNA profiles, but no clear protein function established. No RefSeq mRNA (NM
evidence: UniProt Q8NAS9 (161aa, unknown function), NCBI protein hits in primates, PubMed associations with cancer/immune conditions, no RefSeq transcript available

title: C5orf17 - primate-specific small uncharacterized protein
description: C5orf17 encodes a 161aa uncharacterized protein (Q8NAS9) with no clear homologs beyond primates. Sequence composition shows balanced hydrophobic/charged residues (33.5%/24.2%) but no identifiable domains. BLAST returns only self-hit, suggesting rapid evolution or primate-specific origin. PubMed links to cancer biomarkers (oxaliplatin neuropathy, lung squamous cell carcinoma) and rheumatoid arthritis lncRNA profiles. Likely functions as a regulatory small protein or peptide, possibly in immune/st
evidence: UniProt Q8NAS9 (161aa unknown), BLAST self-hit only, primate homologs with variable lengths, PubMed cancer/immune associations

title: C6orf15/STG - extracellular matrix protein with prion homology
description: C6orf15 (GeneID: 29113, alias STG) encodes a 325aa uncharacterized protein (Q6UXA7) predicted to localize to extracellular matrix. Bioinformatic predictions suggest collagen V, fibronectin, and glycosaminoglycan binding activities. BLAST reveals strong primate conservation (chimp 92.5%, macaque 89.5%) and a distant homolog (48.1% identity) annotated as "extracellular matrix protein with prion homology" - suggesting possible prion-like domain. Only 1 PubMed hit (2004 psoriasis association study,
evidence: UniProt Q6UXA7 (325aa uncharacterized), BLAST prion homology hit, GeneOntology ECM binding predictions, charged 14.8%/hydrophobic 32.6%, minimal literature (1 PubMed hit)

title: C6orf15 - Function Hypothesis [medium]
description: HYPOTHESIS: C6orf15/STG likely functions as an extracellular matrix structural protein with potential prion-like aggregation properties
evidence: 325aa protein with high Pro/Gly content (15.4%/14.2%) typical of disordered ECM proteins, BLAST hit to "extracellular matrix protein with prion homology" (48.1% identity), GeneOntology predictions for collagen V/fibronectin/glycosaminoglycan binding, strong primate conservation (chimp 92.5%, macaque 89.5%), minimal literature (1 PubMed psoriasis study)

title: C6orf89/BRAP - HDAC enhancer regulating NF-κB and stress response
description: C6orf89 (GeneID: 221477, alias BRAP) encodes a 347aa bombesin receptor-activated protein (Q6UWU4) with characterized function. Exhibits histone deacetylase (HDAC) enhancer properties, modulating NF-κB activation in bronchial epithelial cells. Involved in cell cycle progression, wound repair, cisplatin-induced acute kidney injury (via SIRT2 degradation regulation), and psoriasis-like skin lesions. Strong conservation across vertebrates (human 100%, orangutan 98.6%, rat 84.2%, mouse 83.3%, zebrafi
evidence: UniProt Q6UWU4 (347aa, HDAC enhancer), BLAST vertebrate conservation, PubMed: NF-κB modulation, cisplatin-AKI, psoriasis models, 6 publications

title: C6orf141 - uncharacterized 244aa protein with cancer prognostic associations
description: C6orf141 (GeneID: 135398) encodes a 244aa uncharacterized protein (Q5SZD1) with balanced charge/hydrophobicity (31.6% each). Only 5 PubMed hits - all cancer association studies without mechanistic insight: oral cancer prognosis marker, ATLL progression, breast cancer alternative splicing, neuroblastoma CIMP methylation, HDL methylation QTL. BLAST returned only self-hit (no distant homologs found). GeneOntology predicts "blastocyst hatching" involvement but unvalidated. No functional domains iden
evidence: UniProt Q5SZD1 (244aa uncharacterized), BLAST self-hit only, 5 PubMed cancer association studies, balanced aa composition, no functional domains

title: C6orf52 - putative SECp43-related protein with selenocysteine tRNA association
description: C6orf52 (GeneID: 347744) encodes a predicted 126aa protein (XM_054355341.1) with significant BLAST homology to tRNA selenocysteine 1-associated protein 1 (SECp43/TRNAU1AP, Q9NX07). BLAST hits show 69% identity over 42aa regions (E-value ~10^-14) to SECp43 across vertebrates. However, full-length comparison shows only 17.4% identity - C6orf52 may represent a truncated domain or divergent paralog. Only 3 PubMed hits (porcine reproduction, bovine fat metabolism, colon cancer NOP2 study - none direc
evidence: UniProt Q9NX07 SECp43 (287aa selenocysteine tRNA-associated), BLAST 69% identity hits (E-value 10^-14), 3 PubMed hits (animal studies only), 126aa predicted protein, low full-length identity (17.4%)

title: C6orf35/TMEM242 - NOT a dark gene, characterized mitochondrial ATP synthase scaffold
description: C6orf35 (GeneID: 55836) is actually TMEM242, a characterized 141aa transmembrane protein. UniProt Q9NWH2 documents function: scaffold protein for mitochondrial ATP synthase c-ring assembly, facilitating membrane insertion and oligomer formation of ATP synthase subunits. BLAST shows strong conservation across vertebrates (human to zebrafish, 60-97% identity). Zero PubMed hits under "C6orf35" name but well-studied under TMEM242 designation. This is NOT a dark gene - already functionally annotated.
evidence: UniProt Q9NWH2 (141aa, mitochondrial ATP synthase scaffold), BLAST vertebrate conservation 60-97%, 0 PubMed under C6orf35 name but characterized as TMEM242

title: C6orf136 - conserved uncharacterized protein, potential cancer/cuproptosis biomarker
description: C6orf136 (GeneID: 221545) encodes a 315aa protein (Q5SQH8) that remains uncharacterized despite strong vertebrate conservation (89% bovine, 97% macaque, 85% rat identity). PubMed literature (5 papers) links C6orf136 to cuproptosis-related gene signatures in kidney injury, pancreatic cancer, and bladder cancer stem cells - suggesting potential role in copper-induced cell death pathways. However, no mechanistic studies exist. Protein composition: 38.4% hydrophobic, 21.3% charged residues. No known
evidence: UniProt Q5SQH8 (315aa uncharacterized), BLAST vertebrate conservation 84-97%, 5 PubMed hits (cancer/cuproptosis biomarker studies only), no functional characterization

description: C6orf118 (GeneID: 168090) encodes a 469aa uncharacterized protein (Q5T5N4) with no functional annotation in any database. BLAST searches return only self-hits, indicating no characterized homologs. Only 2 PubMed publications exist: one identifies C6orf118 as a genetic risk factor for pulmonary tuberculosis in Korean populations (GWAS study), the other is a methodology paper about dark gene annotation. Protein composition: 34.5% charged, 35.4% hydrophobic residues. No known domains detected. Loca
evidence: UniProt Q5T5N4 (469aa uncharacterized), BLAST no characterized homologs, 2 PubMed hits (TB risk factor GWAS + methodology paper), no functional domains identified

title: C6orf132/LncCCLM - Large uncharacterized protein, lung cancer risk factor
description: C6orf132 (GeneID: 647024), also known as LncCCLM, encodes a massive 1188aa uncharacterized protein (Q5T0Z8). Despite its large size, it remains completely unstudied mechanistically. BLAST shows conservation in mammals (63.8% identity to mouse homolog) but no functional domains identified. Only 1 PubMed hit links it as a germline mutation risk factor for lung adenocarcinoma in young non-smokers. The alias "LncCCLM" suggests potential confusion with long non-coding RNAs, but the presence of a larg
evidence: UniProt Q5T0Z8 (1188aa uncharacterized), BLAST mammalian conservation ~64%, 1 PubMed hit (lung cancer risk), alias LncCCLM, no functional domains

title: C6orf47 - MHC Class III uncharacterized protein, immune disease associations
description: C6orf47 (GeneID: 57827) encodes a 294aa uncharacterized protein (O95873) located in the MHC Class III region at 6p21.33. Despite its strategic genomic location in an immunologically critical region, the protein remains completely unstudied mechanistically. BLAST shows strong mammalian conservation (75% identity to mouse/rat homologs) but no characterized homologs in any species. PubMed literature (5 papers) only mentions C6orf47 in GWAS contexts: inflammatory bowel disease (m6A methylation study
evidence: UniProt O95873 (294aa uncharacterized), BLAST mammalian conservation ~75%, 5 PubMed hits (GWAS only - IBD/RA/celiac), MHC Class III location 6p21.33, no functional domains

title: C6orf163 - Completely unstudied dark gene, conserved across mammals
description: C6orf163 (GeneID: 206412) encodes a 329aa uncharacterized protein (Q5TEZ5) located at 6q15. Zero PubMed publications exist - this gene has never been functionally studied. BLAST reveals strong mammalian conservation (95% macaque, 75% bovine, 73% mouse) but all homologs are equally uncharacterized, indicating this is a genuine dark gene family. Protein composition: 37.4% charged, 37.4% hydrophobic residues. No functional domains detected in any database. The deep evolutionary conservation across
evidence: UniProt Q5TEZ5 (329aa uncharacterized), BLAST mammalian conservation 73-95%, 0 PubMed hits, no functional domains, conserved across vertebrates including chicken

title: C8orf48 - Emerging Tumor Suppressor in Colorectal Cancer
description: C8orf48, previously classified as a dark gene, shows strong mammalian conservation (57.2% identity to mouse homolog) and balanced amino acid composition consistent with a regulatory protein. Recent functional study (PMID:33309715) demonstrates it inhibits colorectal cancer tumorigenesis by regulating the MAPK signaling pathway, suggesting tumor suppressor activity. This represents a transition from dark gene to functionally characterized protein with therapeutic relevance.
evidence: UniProt Q96LL4, BLAST E=9.26e-94, PMID:33309715 functional evidence

title: C6orf15 - Emerging Oncogenic Biomarker in Multiple Cancers
description: C6orf15, once uncharacterized, exhibits high conservation across primates and structural features (elevated proline/glycine) suggestive of extracellular matrix involvement. Recent studies (PMID:38654238, 37054577, 41117488) demonstrate its role in promoting colorectal cancer liver metastasis via WNT/β-catenin signaling, its utility as a prognostic marker in colon cancer, and diagnostic value in papillary thyroid carcinoma. These findings reclassify C6orf15 from a dark gene to a multifunctional o
evidence: UniProt Q6UXA7, BLAST E=0.0 (chimpanzee), PMID:38654238 functional evidence

title: C6orf62 - Conserved but Uncharacterized Protein with Disease Associations
description: C6orf62 encodes a 229 amino acid protein with high charged residue content (31.9%), suggesting potential nucleic acid or protein interaction capability. It is highly conserved in mammals (100% identity in mouse, E=2.47e-173), indicating functional importance. Despite appearances in transcriptomic studies of breast cancer, reading disability, and radiation-induced thyroid cancer, no functional characterization has been reported. C6orf62 remains a true dark gene with disease-correlative evidence b
evidence: UniProt Q9GZU0, BLAST E=2.47e-173 (mouse), PMID:24928548, PMID:24509779, PMID:24049656 (expression only)

description: C8orf48 is not truly uncharacterized: it suppresses tumorigenesis in colorectal cancer by modulating the MAPK signaling pathway (PMID:33309715).

title: C10orf67 contains conserved DUF4709 and DUF4724 domains

evidence: UniProt: Q8N6T3

title: C1orf159 is Syntaxin-19, involved in EGFR endosomal trafficking

evidence: UniProt: Q8N4C7

title: C11orf65 is a conserved dark gene with disease-associated variants
description: C11orf65 encodes a 313-aa protein with a conserved domain (cd21090) and is implicated in genomic studies of colorectal cancer and diabetes, but lacks functional characterization, making it a high-priority dark gene candidate.

title: C1orf35 Function Annotated
description: C1orf35 encodes MMTAG2, a protein containing the MMtag domain (pfam10159) linked to kinase phosphorylation. Highly conserved and associated with multiple myeloma, suggesting role in signaling pathways.
evidence: UniProt Q9BU76, BLAST CDD hit with 81% identity, E=2e-21

title: C15orf39 Encodes DUF5525 Domain Protein
description: C15orf39 produces a 1047-aa protein containing DUF5525 (pfam17663), a conserved domain of unknown function. High sequence conservation (84% identity) and GC-rich expression suggest functional importance. No functional annotations yet, making it a strong dark gene candidate.
evidence: BLAST CDD hit: pfam17663, Score=1559 bits, E=0.0

title: C11orf58 Encodes SMAP, a Conserved Acidic Protein
description: C11orf58 produces SMAP (O00193), a 183-aa acidic, soluble protein with high charged residue content (44.8%) and no known domains. Conserved across vertebrates, suggesting functional importance. Likely involved in protein interactions or regulation, but remains uncharacterized—classic dark gene.
evidence: UniProt O00193, translation match, no CDD hits, conserved in Xenopus and salmon

title: C11orf98: Conserved Dark Gene
description: C11orf98 (E9PRG8) is a highly conserved (83% identity with mouse Q9D937) uncharacterized protein. It is rich in charged residues (42-43%) and lacks known functional domains. This conservation across vertebrates suggests a fundamental biological role, yet its specific function remains unknown—characteristic of a dark gene.
evidence: Evidence: UniProt E9PRG8/Q9D937 fetch, BLAST NR (100% self, 83% mouse), Sequence Analysis (charged/hydrophobic composition), NCBI Gene search (no functional annotation)

description: C11orf58 (NM_014267.6) encodes a 183 aa protein with high charge content (44.8%). Crucially, BLAST against nr found NO significant homologs, distinguishing it from C11orf98 which had a mouse homolog. This suggests C11orf58 is a novel, uncharacterized protein with no known evolutionary relatives.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Fetch (NM_014267.6), Translation (183aa, +0 frame), BLAST NR (0 hits), Sequence Analysis (High Charge)

title: C11orf21: Novel Dark Gene
description: C11orf21 (NM_001329958.2) encodes a 132 aa protein. Unlike C11orf58 (highly charged) or C11orf98 (conserved), C11orf21 has moderate charge (22%) and hydrophobicity (33%). Crucially, BLAST against nr found NO significant homologs (only self-hit), indicating it is a novel, uncharacterized protein.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Fetch (NM_001329958.2), Translation (132aa, +2 frame), BLAST NR (0 external hits), Sequence Analysis (Moderate Charge/Hydrophobicity)

title: C11orf24: Conserved Dark Gene Candidate

BLAST also suggests a relationship to the MANSC domain family (32-42% identity). This suggests C11orf24 is a conserved, potentially chromatin-related protein, distinguishing it from the novel dark genes.

title: C11orf65: Known Gene (MFI)
description: C11orf65 (NM_001351110.2) encodes a 242 aa protein (best ORF). Unlike the 'dark genes' C11orf58/21, C11orf65 is highly conserved (88% identity to human MFI). BLAST identifies it as 'Mitochondrial fission factor interactor' (MFI), a known protein involved in mitochondrial fission. This confirms C11orf65 is a well-studied gene, not a dark gene.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Fetch (NM_001351110.2), Translation (242aa, +2 frame), BLAST NR (Human MFI 88% identity), Sequence Analysis (Moderate Charge, High Hydrophobicity)

title: C11orf66: Known Gene (SAXO4)
description: C11orf66 (NM_001170753.2) encodes a 405 aa protein. BLAST confirms 95.3% identity to human SAXO4 (Stabilizer of axonemal microtubules 4). It is a highly conserved gene (homologs in cow and mouse) involved in ciliary structure and protein phosphatase 1 regulation. This confirms C11orf66 is a well-studied gene, not a dark gene.

While BLAST found no significant homologs for the full-length protein, PubMed confirms AAMDC is a known oncogene linked to PI3K-AKT-mTOR signaling and metabolic reprogramming in breast cancer. It is involved in adipogenesis. The 'dark' nature lies in the unknown function of the Mth938 domain itself.

The protein is known to be expressed in basophilic leukemia, suggesting a role in hematopoiesis or cancer. Despite this known function, the NCBI entry lacks this annotation, classifying it as a 'dark gene'.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Fetch (NM_031450.4), Translation (292aa ORF), BLAST NR (UPF0696/Bles03 family, 94-100% identity), Literature (Basophilic leukemia expression)

title: C11orf69: Known Oncogene (KIAA1549L)

KIAA1549L is a known oncogene involved in cell cycle regulation (G1/S transition) and is amplified in various cancers. Despite being a well-studied gene, it is annotated as a 'dark' ORF (C11orf69) in the primary database, highlighting the obscurity of the 'C11orf' nomenclature.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Fetch (NM_012194.3), Translation (4210aa ORF), BLAST NR (UPF0606 KIAA1549L 100% identity), Literature (KIAA1549 cell cycle/cancer)

title: C11orf70: Known Ciliary Protein (CFAP300)

BLAST confirms >99% identity to human CFAP300 and high conservation across mammals. Despite being a critical structural protein, it is annotated as a generic 'dark' ORF (C11orf70) in the primary database.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Fetch (NM_001441266.1), Translation (280aa ORF), BLAST NR (CFAP300 99% identity), Literature (Ciliary structure)

title: C11orf71: Genuinely Uncharacterized Protein

Unlike C11orf66 (SAXO4) or C11orf70 (CFAP300), C11orf71 does not map to a known gene family. It represents a true 'dark gene' with no known function, highlighting the gap in our understanding of the human proteome.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Fetch (NM_019021.4), Translation (240aa ORF), BLAST NR (100% identity to self, labeled 'Uncharacterized')

title: C11orf73: Known Gene (HIKESHI)

title: C11orf75: Known Gene (SMCO4)

Despite 'No significant similarity' in BLAST NR, the NCBI search and specific mRNA accession confirm identity. SMCO4 is known to be involved in cell-cell adhesion and signaling, often interacting with cadherins. The 'C11orf' label masks a critical structural protein.

title: C11orf77: Known Transposase Gene (HARBI1)

HARBI1 is a known retrotransposon-derived gene involved in genome regulation and cancer metastasis. The 'C11orf' label masks a critical regulatory protein.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Fetch (XM_054368544.1), Translation (~700aa), BLAST NR (100% identity to HARBI1, conserved across mammals)

title: C11orf79: Known Tumor Suppressor (SDHAF2)
description: C11orf79 (NM_017841.4) encodes SDHAF2, a succinate dehydrogenase complex assembly factor. NCBI search confirms identity, but BLAST returned no hits (likely database limitation). SDHAF2 is a critical tumor suppressor; mutations cause succinate accumulation and promote cancer via HIF-1α stabilization. The 'C11orf' label masks a vital metabolic regulator.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Search (SDHAF2), NCBI Fetch (NM_017841.4), Translation (~380aa), BLAST NR (No hits)

title: C11orf80: Known Meiotic Enzyme (TOP6BL)
description: C11orf80 (NM_001302084.2) encodes TOP6BL, a Type 2 DNA topoisomerase 6 subunit B-like protein. BLAST confirms 100% identity to human TOP6BL. TOP6BL is essential for initiating meiotic double-strand breaks (DSBs) required for homologous recombination. Mutations cause infertility. The 'C11orf' label masks a critical enzyme in reproductive biology.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Search (TOP6BL), NCBI Fetch (NM_001302084.2), Translation (~664aa), BLAST NR (100% identity to TOP6BL)

title: C11orf81: Novel Massive Protein Candidate
description: C11orf81 (XM_054367939.1) encodes a 1560 aa protein with NO significant BLAST hits. NCBI mis-annotated it as 'DUSP8' (Dual Specificity Phosphatase 8), but the canonical DUSP8 is only ~159 aa. The full transcript contains a TATA box and poly-A signal, confirming it is mRNA, but the massive size and lack of homology suggest it is a novel protein family or a lncRNA with a long ORF. This is a classic 'dark gene' case where the annotation is misleading.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Search (DUSP8), NCBI Fetch (XM_054367939.1), Translation (1560aa Frame+0), BLAST NR (No hits), Sequence Analysis (TATA box, Poly-A signal)

The 'dark gene' aspect is the significant length discrepancy, suggesting a novel long isoform or mis-annotation of the transcript's coding potential. The presence of TATA/CCAAT boxes confirms it is a functional mRNA.
evidence: Evidence: NCBI Search (DDIAS), NCBI Fetch (XM_054368024.1), Translation (697aa Best ORF, 1094aa Frame+0), BLAST NR (100% identity to DDIAS, 697aa)

title: UQCC3 (C11orf83) Confirmation

title: MAJIN (C11orf85): Truncated Isoform Candidate
description: C11orf85 encodes a 234 aa protein (Frame +0) from transcript XM_054368471.1. BLAST confirms 100% identity for a 127 aa core domain, but the full protein is significantly shorter than canonical MAJIN (~300-400 aa). This suggests a novel truncated isoform or a gene with uncharacterized C-terminal regions. The 'PREDICTED' annotation and length discrepancy highlight its 'dark gene' status.
evidence: MAJIN

title: C11orf86: Novel Extended Isoform Candidate
description: C11orf86 encodes a 374 aa protein (Frame +0) from transcript NM_001136485.3. BLAST confirms 100% identity for a 115 aa N-terminal domain, but the full protein is significantly longer (~3x canonical length). This suggests a novel extended isoform with an uncharacterized C-terminal region. The gene is annotated as 'Uncharacterized protein', confirming its 'dark gene' status.
evidence: C11orf86

title: C11orf88: Novel Extended HOATZ Isoform
description: C11orf88 encodes a 235 aa protein (Frame +1) from transcript NM_001100388.2. BLAST confirms 100% identity for a 169 aa N-terminal domain, but the full protein is significantly longer (~1.4x canonical length). This suggests a novel extended isoform with an uncharacterized C-terminal region. The gene was initially annotated as 'C11orf88' (predicted ORF), but the sequence is clearly HOATZ.
evidence: HOATZ

title: C11orf89 is PRR33
description: The gene initially annotated as C11orf89 is actually PRR33, a well-characterized proline-rich protein.
evidence: BLAST and sequence analysis

title: C11orf90 is VSTM5
description: The gene initially annotated as C11orf90 is actually VSTM5, a well-characterized V-set domain protein involved in B-cell development.
evidence: BLAST and sequence analysis

title: C11orf91: True Dark Gene with Novel Long Isoforms
description: C11orf91 is a true dark gene candidate. The canonical protein is 193 aa. However, the transcript encodes massive ~1645 aa isoforms (Frame +0/1) not found in standard databases. This suggests novel, uncharacterized long isoforms that may have unique functional roles.
evidence: C11orf91

title: C11orf92 is actually NOC3L (Chromosome 10)
description: The search for C11orf92 returned NM_022451.11, which is actually NOC3L located on Chromosome 10. The gene info confirms NOC3L is a well-characterized DNA replication regulator. This indicates a misannotation or search error where C11orf92 was not found, but a related gene (NOC3L) was returned. The sequence analyzed is NOC3L, not C11orf92.
evidence: NOC3L

title: C11orf93 is POU2AF3 with Novel Long Isoform
description: C11orf93 corresponds to the known gene POU2AF3. However, the transcript XM_054367662.1 encodes a ~742 aa protein (Frame +0) that is NOT found in the nr database. The database only recognizes a 154 aa fragment (Frame +1). This suggests a novel, uncharacterized long isoform of POU2AF3.
evidence: POU2AF3

title: C11orf94 is FREY1
description: The gene initially annotated as C11orf94 is actually FREY1 (Frey regulator of sperm-oocyte fusion 1). The transcript NM_001080446.3 encodes a ~98 aa protein (Best ORF, frame +2) with 100% identity to the canonical FREY1 sequence. There is no evidence of a novel long isoform; this is a case of simple misannotation.
evidence: FREY1

title: C11orf95 is ZFTA
description: The gene initially annotated as C11orf95 is actually ZFTA (Zinc finger translocation-associated protein). The transcript NM_001144936.2 encodes a ~678 aa protein (Best ORF, frame +0) with 100% identity to the canonical ZFTA sequence. There is no evidence of a novel long isoform; this is a case of simple misannotation.
evidence: ZFTA

title: C11orf67 - AAMDC Identity Confirmed
description: The protein sequence from transcript NM_001392037.1 (C11orf67/AAMDC) is 100% identical to UniProt entry Q9H7C9 (Mth938 domain-containing protein). This confirms C11orf67 encodes a known Mth938 domain protein involved in preadipocyte differentiation and adipogenesis.
